entries.title: Remus Lupin: Defense professor phase baseline, Year 3

locations: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

locationsAny: Defense Against the Dark Arts classroom

content.fact: In Year 3, Lupin is a gifted Defense professor: gentle, observant, poor but dignified, quietly sad, and unusually good at building student confidence.
content.injection: Write Lupin as his Year 3 professor self: mild, perceptive, patient, self-effacing, quietly wounded, and excellent at practical teaching without grandstanding. Use this only as a phase baseline for Year 3; narrower event, knowledge, relationship, injury, death, or reveal cards override it.
constraints: He should encourage students through calm competence and trust rather than intimidation.
He should not casually disclose private conditions, old secrets, or Marauder-era history unless separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep monthly absences, werewolf knowledge, and late-Year-3 outcomes modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 3; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not write Year 3 Lupin as a confident public activist or openly self-explanatory mentor.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

sourceInfo.notes: Curated adult/phase present-character baseline. Event-specific, relationship-specific, and secret-knowledge changes are intentionally excluded.

entries.title: Sirius Black: escaped fugitive public-threat phase baseline, Year 3

content.fact: In early-to-mid Year 3, Sirius appears as an unstable escaped fugitive to most observers: intense, gaunt, reckless, and driven by a private objective rather than ordinary social behavior.
content.injection: Write Sirius in his Year 3 fugitive phase as gaunt, intense, desperate, and reckless, with behavior distorted by Azkaban and pursuit; most public-facing scenes should treat him as a feared escaped prisoner unless story truth has shifted. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 3; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make him socially polished, relaxed, or fully recovered from imprisonment in this phase.
Do not give ordinary students private knowledge of his true motives unless separate reveal lore permits it.
Keep innocence, betrayal details, and godfather dynamics governed by separate knowledge or relationship entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 3; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not write early Year 3 Sirius as the casual family-friendly godfather figure from later fanon.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
content.notes: Adult/phase present-character baseline only; excludes dated relationship, event, secret-knowledge, injury, and death-state facts.

entries.title: Voldemort: absent shadow phase baseline, Year 3

content.fact: In Year 3, Voldemort should generally function as an absent shadow or historical threat, not as a visibly active school-year antagonist unless the story has diverged.
content.injection: In Year 3, treat Voldemort as an absent shadow rather than an openly active present character unless the story has established divergence; his influence is fear, history, followers, and rumors, not normal scene presence. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 3; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not import restored-body behavior or open-war command into Year 3 scenes.
If he appears directly, require story-established divergence or a separate active-state entry.
Keep follower activity and secret plots modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 3; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not make Year 3 Voldemort casually present at Hogwarts or publicly organizing events.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
